# AML.T0043.000 — White-Box Optimization

**Sub-technique of:** AML.T0043 (Craft Adversarial Data)

## Description

In White-Box Optimization, the adversary has full access to the target model and optimizes the adversarial example directly.
Adversarial examples trained in this manner are most effective against the target model.

## Mitigations

- **Model Hardening** (AML.M0003): Use techniques to make AI models robust to adversarial inputs such as adversarial training or network distillation.
- **Control Access to AI Models and Data at Rest** (AML.M0005): Establish access controls on internal model registries and limit internal access to production models. Limit access to training data only to approved users.
- **Use Ensemble Methods** (AML.M0006): Use an ensemble of models for inference to increase robustness to adversarial inputs. Some attacks may effectively evade one model or model family but be ineffective against others.
- **Input Restoration** (AML.M0010): Preprocess all inference data to nullify or reverse potential adversarial perturbations.
- **Adversarial Input Detection** (AML.M0015): Detect and block adversarial inputs or atypical queries that deviate from known benign behavior, exhibit behavior patterns observed in previous attacks or that come from potentially malicious IPs.
Incorporate adversarial detection algorithms into the AI system prior to the AI model.
- **AI Model Distribution Methods** (AML.M0017): Deploying AI models to edge devices can increase the attack surface of the system.
Consider serving models in the cloud to reduce the level of access the adversary has to the model.
Also consider computing features in the cloud to prevent gray-box attacks, where an adversary has access to the model preprocessing methods.
